response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise across 20+ years, they do not meet the required native-level Japanese proficiency and lack critical embedded networking experience. Their background is predominantly web/application development, with only dated embedded systems exposure (2007–2008). The role demands deep expertise in LAN-switch firmware, security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), and networking infrastructure (NETCONF, SNMP, Telemetry), none of which appear in the resume.
strengths: 20+ years of professional software development demonstrating sustained technical growth and ability to master diverse languages and domains
Strong emphasis on testing, software craftsmanship, and clean code practices shown across multiple roles and projects
Some C/C++ and embedded systems experience from National Instruments role, plus proven Linux proficiency
concerns: Missing required native-level Japanese language proficiency—a stated mandatory qualification for this role
No demonstrated experience with embedded firmware, LAN switches, network switch operation, or embedded product development lifecycle
No mention of security technologies (encryption, key exchange, 802.1X, MACsec) or networking protocols (NETCONF, SNMP, Telemetry, Ansible) essential for the role

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or firmware background
Strong foundational software engineering and learning capability
